proposals.caveat: Requires sufficient pre-event, non-overlapping weeks per city to estimate clean pre-trends.
proposals.question: Do spending levels already trend upward in the one to two weeks before game weeks within the same city, suggesting residual confounding?
proposals.rationale: A placebo-style lead test on the city-week panel using event flags and dates can reveal whether game timing aligns with pre-existing spending upswings.

proposals.caveat: Thresholds may need relaxing if too few MSAs meet the criteria.
proposals.question: Which MSAs have at least 8 event weeks and 8 comparable baseline weeks in the study window to support stable event-versus-non-event contrasts?
proposals.rationale: Counting event and baseline weeks directly from the MSA-week panel establishes readiness for credible comparisons and variance control.

proposals.caveat: Assumes per-city weekly game counts can be derived from the event schedule.
proposals.question: Do weeks with multiple games hosted in the same city yield larger spending lifts than single-game weeks?
proposals.rationale: Comparing spending by event-intensity tiers within city-weeks tests a dose–response pattern using only the local weekly event data.
